column1: # required_fixes.tsv -- the launch-blocking fixes every cut's ARTIFACTS must contain.
# Read by scripts/provenance_gate.sh. Each row names ONE fix commit that MUST be
# proven present -- by ancestry AND by baked-in content -- in a shipped artifact.
# This is the single list that drives content verification, so a merged fix can
# no longer slip the cut because a human forgot to add a wiki_image_grep marker.
# THE CLASS THIS KILLS
# A fix merges to a source main; the artifact (Docker image / daemon / vendor) is
# re-pinned; a ledger row is recorded claiming the new source sha. Every existing
# gate then trusts that CLAIM. Nothing checks the digest was ACTUALLY built from
# that sha (the image has no revision label -- proven). This file + the gate grep
# INSIDE the real artifact for a marker that exists ONLY with the fix, so a false
# ledger row (right sha recorded, old content baked) is caught.
# Format (TAB-separated, 6 fields; '#' = comment, '#TODO ' = surfaced reminder):
#   repo  fix_commit  artifact  content_marker  marker_path  description
#   repo           CM044 | ostler-assistant | CM051  (ancestry resolution)
#   fix_commit     the launch-required commit SHA (short ok)
#   artifact       wiki-compiler | wiki-site | daemon | vendor:<path-rel-CM051>
#   content_marker distinctive regex introduced BY the fix -- MUST be verified
#                  present-at-fix AND absent-before-fix on a real artifact before
#                  encoding (the same discipline cut_markers.manifest documents).
#   marker_path    path to grep inside the artifact (image path / daemon path)
#   description    human note (PR number etc.)
# v1.0.10 CM044 wiki fixes (648f7491 tip). Markers verified present in the pinned
# images (compiler 2f7b0b73 / site 563fbcec) AND absent in the pre-#146 build
# a7e8c7e8 (built from 77300f6 = the parent of f936095) -- so each discriminates.
CM044
CM044
CM044
